name: Generate invoices with HTTP / REST API and Notion end to end

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03576-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Gather API Numbers

### Gather API Numbers

GET https://api.example.com/v1/documents — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Blend Sources

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Gather from Notion

Pulls every page from the Notion database — fine for small datasets; add a filter if the database grows large.

Next: Blend Sources

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Blend Sources

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Roll Up KPIs

### Roll Up KPIs

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Compose HTML Report

### Compose HTML Report

Custom JavaScript: Build an HTML digest table from the aggregated rows

Next: Send Report via Slack

### Send Report via Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Generate invoices with HTTP / REST API and Notion end to end

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Generate invoices with HTTP / REST API and Notion end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual ocr & extraction is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Gather API Numbers → Blend Sources → Roll Up KPIs → Compose HTML Report → Send Report via Slack → Stamp Processing Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating ocr & extraction in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

KPIs are rolled up with Summarize (exact group-by + aggregate) before any AI touches the data — asking an LLM to 'figure out the numbers' from raw records is slower, costlier, and less reliable than doing exact arithmetic in n8n and only asking the model to narrate numbers that are already correct.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a second data source and watch it blend into the same report via Merge.
2. Toggle the AI executive-summary step on and off and compare the report's usefulness.
3. Change the KPI grouping field and see how the pivot reshapes.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your ocr & extraction process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
